title: Cozy Cheesy British Sausages Toad In The Hole
description: A hearty, cheesy British dish featuring sausages cooked in a yellow masarepa crust, perfect for a cozy night in. This shojin-friendly recipe is slow-cooked to perfection.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the yellow masarepa, melted butter, and a pinch of salt.
Gradually add the milk to the bowl, stirring until a smooth batter forms.
Add the shredded cheese, chopped onion, and a pinch of pepper to the bowl, stirring until well combined.
Crack in the eggs and stir until the batter is smooth and creamy.
Pour the batter into a greased 9x13 inch baking dish.
Make 4 holes in the batter, spaced evenly apart, and place a sausage in each hole.
Bake the toad in the hole for 35-40 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the sausages are cooked through.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the cozy cheesy British sausages toad in the hole hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
